currentTask.name: Upgrade workflow into a big-goal execution system
currentTask.goal: Preserve large goals through north-star and milestone shaping, keep execution slice-sized, and govern optimization as its own lane

environment.note: This workspace runs entirely on Debian/WSL2. No PowerShell needed.
environment.primaryTerminal: WSL Debian with native Linux tools

whatChanged: Added slice-first workflow guardrails and committed them as e1299cf
Added north-star.sh for long-horizon goal shaping
Added milestone-shape.sh for turning big goals into bounded bets
Added optimize-gate.sh for evidence-based optimization routing
Extended task-intake.sh with north-star goal horizon detection
Updated start-task and shape-task behavior to make shaping the default first move for serious tasks
Updated workflow docs with big-goal execution diagrams and optimization lane guidance
Updated history index and full detailed history to record the workflow evolution
verification: bash ./scripts/north-star.sh "recreate Elemental Battlegrounds faithfully for nostalgia" --no-fetch
bash ./scripts/milestone-shape.sh "recreate Elemental Battlegrounds faithfully for nostalgia" --no-fetch
bash ./scripts/optimize-gate.sh "optimize the architecture for future scale" --evidence predicted --scope architecture
residualRisk: Propagation still needs one more apply pass and topic-repo commits should be handled carefully because some topic repos already have unrelated local work.
immediateNextSteps: Propagate the new north-star, milestone, and optimization lane templates into managed topic repos
Decide whether fluent-prs should receive only the managed refresh or also a checkpoint commit
Optionally simplify older docs that still sound too tied to the earlier subagent-heavy worldview
resumeRules: Read session-state.json first on every resume
